prompt: Which team won the 2022 World Series?
explanation: The Houston Astros beat the Philadelphia Phillies to win the 2022 World Series.

prompt: Which team won the 2021 World Series?
explanation: The Atlanta Braves won their first title since 1995, beating the Houston Astros.

prompt: Which team won the 2020 World Series, played in a neutral-site 'bubble' due to COVID-19?
explanation: The Dodgers won their first title since 1988, beating Tampa Bay in the COVID-affected 2020 season.

prompt: Which pitcher signed a record-breaking contract with the Los Angeles Dodgers before the 2024 season, coming from Japan?
options: Shohei Ohtani (already there as a hitter/pitcher)
explanation: Yoshinobu Yamamoto signed a record contract for a pitcher with the Dodgers ahead of 2024.

prompt: Which pitcher threw a perfect game for the Philadelphia Phillies against the Tampa Bay Rays in the 2010 World Series?
explanation: Roy Halladay threw a no-hitter in Game 1 of the 2010 NLDS, one of just two postseason no-hitters ever.

prompt: Who threw the only perfect game in World Series history, in 1956?
explanation: Don Larsen threw a perfect game for the Yankees against the Dodgers in Game 5 of the 1956 World Series.

prompt: Which team did Don Larsen pitch his perfect game against in the 1956 World Series?
explanation: Larsen's perfect game came against the Brooklyn Dodgers, still the only perfect game in postseason history.

prompt: Which pitcher holds the record for most career no-hitters, with seven?
explanation: Nolan Ryan's seven career no-hitters is an MLB record unlikely to be matched.

prompt: Which pitcher holds the MLB career strikeout record?
explanation: Nolan Ryan struck out 5,714 batters over his career, an MLB record.

prompt: Which team did the Chicago Cubs beat to win the 2016 World Series, ending a 108-year championship drought?
explanation: The Cubs beat Cleveland in a thrilling seven-game series, ending their title drought since 1908.

prompt: What was notable about Game 7 of the 2016 World Series between the Cubs and Cleveland?
options: A rain delay before extra innings preceded the Cubs' win
It ended in regulation with no complications
There was a controversial ejection in the ninth
It was the shortest Game 7 ever played
explanation: A rain delay before the 10th inning became a memorable part of the Cubs' championship-clinching win.

prompt: Which player was named World Series MVP for the Cubs in 2016?
explanation: Ben Zobrist was named World Series MVP for his clutch hitting throughout the series.

prompt: Which team ended a long championship drought by winning the 2004 World Series, their first title since 1918?
explanation: The Red Sox won their first title since 1918, overcoming the so-called 'Curse of the Bambino'.

prompt: What is the nickname for the Boston Red Sox's championship drought, attributed to trading Babe Ruth to the Yankees?
explanation: The 'Curse of the Bambino' referred to the Red Sox's title drought following Babe Ruth's trade to New York in 1919.

prompt: Which team did the Red Sox famously come back from a 0-3 deficit against in the 2004 ALCS, the only such comeback in MLB postseason history?
explanation: Boston's comeback from 0-3 down against the Yankees remains the only such comeback in MLB postseason history.

prompt: Which player hit a walk-off home run to win Game 4 of the 2004 ALCS for the Red Sox, sparking their comeback?
explanation: David Ortiz's walk-off homer in Game 4 kept Boston's season alive during their historic comeback.

prompt: Which player is nicknamed 'Big Papi' and became a Red Sox legend and clutch postseason hitter?
explanation: David Ortiz earned the nickname 'Big Papi' during his years leading Boston to multiple titles.

prompt: Which pitcher famously pitched through a bloody ankle injury in Game 6 of the 2004 ALCS, in what became known as the 'Bloody Sock Game'?
explanation: Curt Schilling's performance despite a torn tendon became one of the most memorable moments in Red Sox history.

prompt: Which team won three World Series titles between 2010 and 2014?
explanation: The Giants won titles in 2010, 2012, and 2014, an unusual even-year pattern.

prompt: What did MLB begin cracking down on in 2021, requiring regular in-game checks of pitchers?
options: Foreign/sticky substances used to improve grip and spin
explanation: MLB introduced regular checks in 2021 to combat pitchers using foreign substances for extra grip and spin.

prompt: Which record did Barry Bonds set in the 2001 season for single-season home runs?
explanation: Barry Bonds hit 73 home runs in 2001, the current single-season MLB record.

prompt: Which two players engaged in a famous home run chase in 1998, both surpassing Roger Maris's single-season record?
options: Barry Bonds and Ken Griffey Jr.
explanation: McGwire (70) and Sosa (66) both surpassed Maris's 61 home runs in a thrilling 1998 season.

prompt: How many home runs did Mark McGwire hit in his record-breaking 1998 season?
explanation: McGwire finished the 1998 season with 70 home runs, a new single-season record at the time.

prompt: Which pitcher won a record seven Cy Young Awards during his career?
explanation: Roger Clemens's seven Cy Young Awards is the most of any pitcher in MLB history.

prompt: Which pitcher won four consecutive Cy Young Awards from 1992 to 1995?
explanation: Greg Maddux's four consecutive Cy Young Awards reflected his sustained dominance in the early-to-mid 1990s.

prompt: Which team did Greg Maddux, Tom Glavine, and John Smoltz all pitch for during the Atlanta Braves' dominant 1990s rotation?
explanation: This trio anchored the Braves' rotation during a run of 14 consecutive division titles.

prompt: How many consecutive division titles did the Atlanta Braves win from 1991 to 2005 (excluding the 1994 strike year)?
explanation: The Braves won 14 consecutive division titles, an unmatched run of sustained regular-season success.

prompt: Despite their dominant regular seasons, how many World Series titles did the 1990s-2000s Atlanta Braves dynasty win?
explanation: Despite their sustained excellence, the Braves won only one World Series (1995) during their run of division titles.

prompt: Which team did the Atlanta Braves beat in the 1999 NLCS before losing the World Series to the Yankees?
explanation: Atlanta beat the Mets in a dramatic six-game NLCS before being swept by the Yankees in the World Series.

prompt: Which team swept the Atlanta Braves in the 1999 World Series?
explanation: The Yankees completed a sweep of the Braves in the 1999 World Series.

prompt: Which player won the American League MVP award in both 2005 and multiple other seasons, known as one of the greatest all-around players of his era?
explanation: Alex Rodriguez won three AL MVP awards during his career, including in 2005 and 2007.

prompt: Which Japanese player set the MLB rookie hits record with 242 hits in 2001?
explanation: Ichiro Suzuki's rookie season with Seattle in 2001 set a modern-era hits record.

prompt: Which team did Ichiro Suzuki play for during his historic MLB debut season in 2001?
explanation: Ichiro won AL MVP and Rookie of the Year in his debut season with the Seattle Mariners.

prompt: Which player holds the single-season MLB hits record with 262, set in 2004?
explanation: Ichiro Suzuki broke George Sisler's 84-year-old record with 262 hits in the 2004 season.

prompt: Which team drafted Derek Jeter and saw him become a franchise icon, winning five World Series titles?
explanation: Derek Jeter spent his entire 20-year career with the Yankees, winning five championships.

prompt: Which trophy is awarded to the All-Star Game MVP, named after a legendary player?
options: Ted Williams Most Valuable Player Award
explanation: The All-Star Game MVP award has been named the Ted Williams Most Valuable Player Award since 2002.

prompt: Which player was the last to hit .400 in a single MLB season, batting .406 in 1941?
explanation: Ted Williams's .406 average in 1941 remains the last time any player hit over .400 in a season.

statement: Don Larsen threw the only perfect game in World Series history.
explanation: Larsen's 1956 perfect game against Brooklyn remains a unique postseason achievement.

explanation: The Cubs' title in 2016 was their first since 1908.

explanation: Boston's title in 2004 was their first since 1918, after trading Babe Ruth decades earlier.

explanation: Bonds set the record during the 2001 season.

statement: Ted Williams was the last MLB player to hit .400 in a season.
explanation: Williams hit .406 in 1941, and no player has matched that mark since.

explanation: No other pitcher has come close to matching Ryan's seven career no-hitters.

explanation: Despite their dominance, the Braves won only one World Series (1995) during that stretch.

explanation: Ortiz earned this nickname during his years as a Red Sox legend.

fixture: World Series Game 5, 1956 (Don Larsen's perfect game)

prompt: Order these World Series championship droughts ending, chronologically (earliest first).
events: Boston Red Sox win their first title since 1918 (2004)
Chicago White Sox win their first title since 1917 (2005)
San Francisco Giants win their first title since moving to SF (2010)
Chicago Cubs win their first title since 1908 (2016)
explanation: Order: Red Sox 2004, White Sox 2005, Giants 2010, Cubs 2016.

prompt: Which pitcher threw a perfect game for the Seattle Mariners against the Tampa Bay Rays in 2012?
explanation: Felix Hernandez, nicknamed 'King Felix', threw a perfect game in August 2012.

prompt: Which pitcher is nicknamed 'King Felix' for his dominant years with Seattle?
explanation: Felix Hernandez spent his entire career with the Seattle Mariners, becoming a franchise icon.

prompt: Which team won the 2019 World Series, becoming the first team to win it entirely on the road?
explanation: The Nationals won all four of their series wins on the road in a historic 2019 World Series.

prompt: Which pitcher led the Washington Nationals to their first World Series title in 2019, later signing a record deal with the Mets?
explanation: Stephen Strasburg was named World Series MVP for the Nationals in 2019.

prompt: Which team's front office pioneered the analytics-driven 'Moneyball' approach in the early 2000s?
explanation: The Oakland Athletics, under GM Billy Beane, pioneered sabermetric approaches chronicled in the book and film 'Moneyball'.

prompt: Which executive is most associated with pioneering the 'Moneyball' analytics strategy for the Oakland Athletics?
explanation: Billy Beane's data-driven approach as Oakland's GM was chronicled in Michael Lewis's book 'Moneyball'.

prompt: Which executive built both the Boston Red Sox's 2004 title team and later the Chicago Cubs' 2016 championship roster?
explanation: Theo Epstein is the only executive to build World Series-winning rosters for two different long-suffering franchises.

prompt: Which player won back-to-back National League MVP awards in 2022 and 2023 for the Atlanta Braves... wait, correct which player won consecutive NL MVPs recently for a different team?
options: Ronald Acuna Jr. won NL MVP in 2023 for Atlanta
explanation: Ronald Acuna Jr. won the 2023 NL MVP after a 40-70 home run/steal season for Atlanta.

prompt: Which player became the first in MLB history to hit 40 home runs and steal 70 bases in the same season, in 2023?
explanation: Acuna's unprecedented 41 home run, 73 stolen base season in 2023 was a historic first.

prompt: Which player won the NL MVP award in 2022 while playing for the St. Louis Cardinals?
explanation: Paul Goldschmidt won his first career MVP award with the Cardinals in 2022.

prompt: Which player won consecutive AL MVP awards in 2019 and 2022 for the Los Angeles Angels?
explanation: Mike Trout has won three career AL MVP awards, cementing his status as one of the game's best players.

prompt: Which player won back-to-back unanimous AL MVP awards in 2021 and 2023 while playing for the Angels?
explanation: Ohtani won his first two MVP awards unanimously in 2021 and 2023 with the Angels, before a third in 2024 with the Dodgers.

prompt: Which player won the 2024 NL MVP award after joining the Dodgers and helping them win the World Series?
explanation: Ohtani won his third career MVP award in 2024, his first with the Dodgers, becoming the first unanimous MVP with a new team in his debut season there.

prompt: Which player was named World Series MVP for the Dodgers in 2024?
explanation: Freddie Freeman was named World Series MVP after a historic performance including a walk-off grand slam in Game 1.

prompt: Which historic feat did Freddie Freeman achieve in Game 1 of the 2024 World Series?
options: A perfect game as a position player
explanation: Freeman's walk-off grand slam was the first ever hit in Game 1 of a World Series.

prompt: Which pitcher won the 2024 NL Cy Young Award pitching for the Atlanta Braves?
explanation: Chris Sale had a resurgent season for Atlanta, winning his first career Cy Young Award in 2024.

prompt: Which pitcher won the 2024 AL Cy Young Award?
explanation: Tarik Skubal won the 2024 AL Cy Young Award pitching for the Detroit Tigers.

prompt: Which Colorado Rockies player hit home runs in eight consecutive games in 2019, tying one of the longest streaks in modern MLB history?
explanation: Trevor Story's eight-game home run streak in 2019 tied one of the longest such streaks in modern history.

prompt: Which pitcher won the World Series MVP award for the Houston Astros in 2022?
explanation: Jeremy Pena, a rookie shortstop, was named World Series MVP for the Astros in 2022.

prompt: Which award did Justin Verlander win in 2022, his third career, tying a record for AL pitchers?
explanation: Verlander won his third Cy Young Award in 2022, matching Roger Clemens's AL record at the time.

prompt: Which team acquired Juan Soto in a blockbuster trade from the Washington Nationals in 2022?
explanation: Juan Soto was traded to the San Diego Padres midway through the 2022 season.

prompt: Which team did Juan Soto sign with as a free agent before the 2025 season, on a record-breaking contract?
explanation: Soto signed a record 15-year, $765 million deal with the New York Mets.

prompt: Which player is known for his elite bat-to-ball skills and consistently high batting averages while playing for the Padres and Nationals?
explanation: Juan Soto's elite plate discipline has made him one of the game's premier hitters since his debut.

prompt: Which player was suspended for performance-enhancing drug use in 2021, missing significant time for the Padres despite being a young star?
explanation: Fernando Tatis Jr. was suspended 80 games in 2022 for a PED violation.

prompt: Roughly how many players in MLB history have hit a home run in their very first career at-bat?
explanation: Hitting a home run in one's first MLB at-bat is rare but has been achieved by more than 100 players throughout history.

prompt: Which award is given annually to a player who has overcome adversity, such as injury or personal hardship, to succeed?
explanation: The Comeback Player of the Year Award recognizes players who overcame significant challenges.

prompt: Which award is given to the best offensive performer in each league, named after a home run legend?
explanation: The Hank Aaron Award recognizes the best offensive player in each league each season.

prompt: Which stadium hosted the first-ever regular-season MLB game played in London, England, in 2019?
explanation: The Yankees and Red Sox played a regular-season series at London Stadium in 2019.

prompt: Which two MLB teams played the historic first regular-season game in London in 2019?
options: New York Yankees and Boston Red Sox
Los Angeles Dodgers and San Francisco Giants
Chicago Cubs and St. Louis Cardinals
New York Mets and Philadelphia Phillies
explanation: The Yankees swept the Red Sox in a two-game series at London Stadium in June 2019.

prompt: Which rule change did MLB introduce in 2023 to speed up the pace of play?
explanation: The pitch clock, introduced in 2023, significantly reduced average game times.

prompt: Which rule limiting defensive positioning was also introduced in MLB starting in 2023?
options: Shift restrictions (infielders must be positioned on either side of second base)
explanation: New shift rules required two infielders on each side of second base, aiming to increase balls in play.
